L. 112–141section 101 of Title 23

children.text: has a driver’s license revoked, suspended, or canceled by a State, has lost the right to operate a commercial motor vehicle in a State, or has been disqualified from operating a commercial motor vehicle; or
has more than one driver’s license (except as allowed under ).section 31302 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: by making an inquiry at least once every 12 months to the appropriate State agency in which the driver held or holds a commercial driver’s license or permit during such time period;

children.label: B
children.text: by receiving occurrence-based reports of changes in the status of a driver’s record from 1 or more driver record notification systems that meet minimum standards issued by the Secretary; or

children.label: C
children.text: by a combination of inquiries to States and reports from driver record notification systems.

children.heading: .—Record keeping
children.label: 2 .—Record keeping
children.text: A copy of the reports received under paragraph (1) shall be maintained in the driver’s qualification file.

children.label: A
children.text: if the employer obtains the driver’s identification number, type, and issuing State of the driver’s commercial motor vehicle license; or

children.label: B
children.text: if the information described in subparagraph (A) is furnished by another employer and the employer that regularly employs the driver meets the other requirements under this section.

children.heading: .—Driver record notification system defined
children.label: 4 .—Driver record notification system defined
children.text: In this section, the term “driver record notification system” means a system that automatically furnishes an employer with a report, generated by the appropriate agency of a State, on the change in the status of an employee’s driver’s license due to a conviction for a moving violation, a failure to appear, an accident, driver’s license suspension, driver’s license revocation, or any other action taken against the driving privilege.
